theme: Surface the Signal — Make Every Pixel Tell a Story

ideas.title: Bottleneck Beacon — Stuck Workers Surface to the Stats Bar
ideas.description: When any worker hits the 'stuck' duration tier (>2h on same task), only the individual card shows it. The stats bar at the top of Dashboard knows nothing. Add a pulsing '⚠️ Bottleneck' stat card that appears dynamically when any worker is stuck — showing their name and duration: '⚠️ Dave · 3h 14m'. Click it to jump and focus their card. Implementation: derive a `stuckWorkers` list from `activeWorkers.filter(w => w.status === 'working' && duration > 2h)` and render a new `.stat-card--stuck` when list is non-empty. WHY: Managers scan the header, not individual cards. A problem that's only visible after scrolling isn't actionable. The stuck tier is already detected — it just needs to be promoted to where decision-makers look first.

ideas.title: Progress Ring — Arc Around the Worker Avatar on Cards
ideas.description: The worker avatar (the character SVG in the card's top-left) sits inside a plain rounded square. Wrap it in a thin SVG progress arc — a circular ring that fills clockwise proportional to `worker.progress`. Color maps to state: green (progressing normally), amber (warn tier), red (stuck/error), grey (idle). The ring is 3px wide, radius fits the avatar container, renders as an SVG overlay. WHY: The progress bar below is great for detail, but you already have to look at it. A ring around the avatar means status is scannable at a glance even at small sizes — the same reason health bars are circles in games. It creates a second, redundant visual encoding that makes the grid legible without reading. Effort: add an SVG wrapper around `.worker-avatar` with a computed `stroke-dashoffset`.

ideas.title: Sync Heartbeat Ripple — The Live Dot Actually Lives
ideas.description: The Live/Offline status dot in the stats bar is completely static. After 5 seconds, nobody can tell if it's a real live signal or a frozen green pixel. Add a CSS ripple animation: every 30s (matching POLL_INTERVAL_ACTIVE) a translucent ring expands outward and fades from the dot — sonar-style. On manual sync button click, fire a fast triple-ripple instead of just spinning the arrow. Implementation: a `::after` pseudo-element on `.live-dot.live` with a keyframe that scales from 1→2.5 and fades to 0. The sync button click adds a `is-syncing` class that plays the triple variant. WHY: Static dots lose meaning instantly. A rhythmic pulse is proof-of-life — it communicates 'I just checked and everything is fine' without any words. Users who leave the tab open should feel like the app is breathing.

ideas.title: Office Wall Clock — A Ticking Analog Clock on the Floor
ideas.description: The office window beautifully simulates time of day (sunrise → dusk → midnight stars), but there's no actual clock on the wall. Add a small analog SVG clock in the top-left area of the office floor near the 'Bloberto's HQ' corner label. Hour and minute hands only, no numbers, minimal design (circle + two lines). Update every minute by piggybacking on the existing `useTimeTick(60_000)` hook — no new intervals needed. Position it as a wall decoration (position: absolute, top 8%, left 6%). WHY: A real office has a wall clock. It's a tiny 40×40px SVG that costs almost nothing but completes the spatial illusion. Right now the time-aware window and the clock-free room feel slightly inconsistent. This closes the loop. It also gives the 'after hours' vibe a subtle melancholy — it's 1am and everyone's gone home.

ideas.title: Daily Sprint Digest — The TLDR Above the Stats Bar
ideas.description: Add a single-line collapsible 'Today' strip between the tab toggle and the stats bar on Dashboard: `✅ 4 completed · ❌ 1 error · 🏆 Maya (3 tasks)`. Derived entirely from `activityLog.filter(e => isToday(e.timestamp))`. Clicking it expands an inline mini-timeline of the day's notable events (just hires and completions). Collapses on click or after 8 seconds. WHY: The activity log tells the full story in chronological detail — but casual visitors and stakeholders just want the headline. 'What happened today?' should be answerable in 2 seconds without scrolling. The data is already there; this is pure presentation work. Also acts as a status trophy for the team: seeing '12 completed today' at the top is motivating.

ideas.title: Mobile Activity Ticker — Fill the Dead Zone Below the Floor
ideas.description: On mobile, there is a large blank area below the office floor visualization (clearly visible in screenshots: the floor ends around 55% of the viewport height, leaving ~200px of empty dark space before the footer). Populate it with a horizontally auto-scrolling activity ticker: the last 5 activity log events scroll left as compact event pills, each showing the worker emoji + abbreviated message. Pauses on touch. Implementation: a `<marquee>`-replacement using CSS `@keyframes ticker-scroll` on a flex row of pill spans. Data from the existing `activityLog` prop. WHY: Dead screen real estate on mobile is a conversion killer. Visitors scrolling on their phone expect content to fill the frame. A live ticker in that zone reinforces that this is a real-time product, not a static page.

ideas.title: Vacant Desk Lore — Empty Desks Should Tell Stories
ideas.description: The vacant desk currently renders '📋 Vacant' and a dim monitor. It is the most sterile element on the entire floor. Instead, randomize between 3 atmospheric variants each session: (1) a sticky note on the monitor reading '📌 Hiring — apply within', (2) a forgotten coffee mug and a tiny withered plant sprite, (3) a keyboard with a sticky note '🔑 left for lunch, brb'. Pick a variant via `Math.floor(Math.random() * 3)` stable per session (seeded by desk index). WHY: Empty desks in real offices are haunting — there's always a trace of whoever sat there. Right now it's blank corporate void. Each variant takes 5 lines of JSX and zero data changes, but transforms the vacant slot from placeholder into lore. It rewards anyone who looks closely. That's the Bloberto spirit.
